drafts.text:
v1.16 shipped this morning.

Jot now transcribes Mandarin, Cantonese, and Vietnamese on-device — joining English, French, Spanish, German, Italian, Portuguese, Dutch, and Japanese. 11 languages, all running on the Apple Neural Engine.

No cloud. No API key. No subscription. $0.

The model is ~1 GB and downloads on first use. Your voice never leaves your Mac.

What language do you work in?

#macOS #indiedev #buildinpublic
drafts.note: Attach bold-claim.svg (export as 1080×1080 PNG). Do NOT put the link in the post body — LinkedIn downranks posts with external links. Paste the utm_url as the FIRST COMMENT immediately after posting. The language question is today's engagement trigger — reply to every multilingual response.

drafts.text: v1.16: Jot now transcribes Mandarin, Cantonese, and Vietnamese on-device. 11 languages total. 0 servers. $0.
drafts.note: Attach bold-claim.svg (export as PNG). No link in post body — post the utm_url as the first reply immediately after posting. ~105 characters. The CJK language callout pulls in non-English-speaking Mac users who've assumed dictation apps don't support their language.

drafts.text:
I added Mandarin, Cantonese, and Vietnamese on-device transcription to my free Mac dictation app (v1.16, I built it)

Just shipped v1.16. Main new thing: Mandarin, Cantonese, and Vietnamese support via Qwen3-ASR running entirely on-device on Apple Silicon.

Background: Jot is a free, open-source Mac dictation app — press a hotkey, speak, transcript lands wherever your cursor is in any app. v1.15 (yesterday) added a movable dictation pill and better search. v1.16 adds CJK languages.

The Qwen3-ASR model is ~1 GB and downloads on first use. No cloud round-trip — your voice stays on your Mac.

Full language list now:
- English (Nemotron on M2 Pro / 16 GB+, Parakeet elsewhere)
- French, Spanish, German, Italian, Portuguese, Dutch (Parakeet v3 multilingual)
- Japanese (Parakeet JA)
- Mandarin, Cantonese, Vietnamese (Qwen3-ASR, Experimental)

A few things worth noting:
- CJK output is native script (Chinese characters, Vietnamese diacritics) — not Pinyin
- The Qwen3-ASR path is batch-only (no live audio preview in the pill)
- Custom vocabulary doesn't apply to the Qwen3-ASR languages yet
- Requires macOS 15+

Apple Silicon only, macOS 15+. Free and open source on GitHub.

(I built this — disclosure)

Link in comments.
drafts.note: Post to r/MacOS (rotating: macapps Jun 23, MacOS Jun 24). First line is the post title. Replace 'Link in comments' with the actual utm_url as the first comment. recommended:false — last recommended:true reddit was June 23 (1 day ago); needs 4+ days — earliest is June 27. Answer every comment in the first hour.

outreach.contact: Contact form at machow2.com (footer — unconfirmed direct email)
outreach.why: One of the highest-ranking 'best dictation software mac' pages in search (2026 edition). Ran a dedicated Voibe review showing they cover indie Mac dictation apps seriously. Current roundup lists Voibe, Superwhisper, Apple Dictation — the only fully free open-source on-device option (Jot) is missing.
outreach.pitch:
Hi,

Your Best Dictation Software for Mac guide is one of the most thorough I've seen — and your dedicated Voibe review shows you dig into indie Mac tools seriously, not just the headline names.

I wanted to flag Jot, which I built. It's free, open-source, and transcribes entirely on-device on the Apple Neural Engine — no cloud, no subscription, no account required. Press a hotkey, speak, and the transcript pastes wherever your cursor is in any app.

I shipped v1.16 this morning adding Mandarin, Cantonese, and Vietnamese support — 11 on-device languages total now. It's the only fully free option in the space, and I think that's a meaningful gap in your roundup for readers who want on-device dictation without paying $8–15/month.

https://jot-transcribe.com/?utm_source=press&utm_medium=outreach&utm_campaign=machow2

Happy to provide screenshots, a demo, or a review DMG.

Vineet

outreach.why: Publishes regularly updated 'Best Mac Voice Dictation Software' roundups — surfaced in today's searches. Covers the exact audience. Jot almost certainly absent. Smaller blog likely to respond to a concise pitch.
outreach.pitch:
Hi,

I came across your Best Mac Voice Dictation Software roundup — you've clearly tested these tools hands-on, which is rare.

I wanted to flag Jot, a free open-source Mac dictation app I've been building since April. Core loop: press a hotkey, speak, transcript pastes wherever your cursor is in any app. Transcription runs entirely on the Apple Neural Engine — no cloud, no account, your voice never leaves the Mac.

I shipped v1.16 today adding Mandarin, Cantonese, and Vietnamese on-device transcription — 11 languages total, still $0. Apple Silicon only, macOS 15+.

https://jot-transcribe.com/?utm_source=press&utm_medium=outreach&utm_campaign=willowvoice

Happy to send screenshots or walk through it.

Vineet

notes: v1.16 (00:44 UTC) and v1.16.1 (04:48 UTC) shipped today — two releases in one day signals a quick follow-on fix. The CJK language expansion (Mandarin, Cantonese, Vietnamese via Qwen3-ASR Experimental) is Jot's first non-Western-alphabet capability and opens a genuinely new audience segment. Total downloads 224 (+18 vs Jun 23's 206, avg7=12/day — new high). At 12/day, the 250-download milestone is ~2 days out. iTunes API 403 (11th+ consecutive day); appstore.json date updated to 2026-06-24, all other values preserved including installs:0. Outreach window reopened (last Jun 19, 5 days). machow2.com and willowvoice.com are both new targets not in prior history. Two Voibe blog comment opportunities remain easy wins. Reddit recommended:false (last true Jun 23; earliest Jun 27). Instagram recommended:false (last true Jun 23; earliest Jun 26). LinkedIn and X both recommended:true. The bold-claim card format last ran Jun 16 — today's '11 languages.' is a fresh number with a different emotional register (breadth vs purity). The Mandarin/Cantonese storyboard is Jot's most visually differentiated clip to date — record while v1.16 is news.
